src/api/getRecords.ts.content:
import { z } from 'zod';
import { createEndpoint, Database } from 'zite-integrations-backend-sdk';

const rowSchema = z.object({
  id: z.number(),
  orderId: z.string().optional(),
  dateOfLive: z.string().optional(),
  page: z.string().optional(),
  liverName: z.string().optional(),
  locationOfMiner: z.string().optional(),
  minerName: z.string().optional(),
  itemDescription: z.string().optional(),
  grams: z.number().optional(),
  source: z.string().optional(),
  category: z.string().optional(),
  mc: z.string().optional(),
  goldRate: z.number().optional(),
  supplierRate: z.number().optional(),
  clientRate: z.number().optional(),
  profit: z.number().optional(),
  modeOfSale: z.string().optional(),
  status: z.string().optional(),
  modeOfPayment: z.string().optional(),
  downpayment: z.string().optional(),
  la1MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la2MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la3MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la4MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  liverAdminRemarks: z.string().optional(),
  dispatchDate: z.string().optional(),
  deliveredDate: z.string().optional(),
  reviewChasing: z.string().optional(),
  fbProfileName: z.string().optional(),
  regions: z.string().optional(),
  remittanceStatus: z.string().optional(),
  currency: z.string().optional(),
  additionalCharges: z.string().optional(),
  amountReceived: z.string().optional(),
  freeSf: z.boolean().optional(),
  auditTrail: z.string().optional(),
  tog: z.string().optional(),
  pureWeight: z.string().optional(),
  customerId: z.string().optional(),
});

export default createEndpoint({
  description: 'Fetches all records from the Database sheet in batches',
  inputSchema: z.object({}),
  outputSchema: z.array(rowSchema),
  execute: async () => {
    const BATCH_SIZE = 500;
    const allRecords: z.infer<typeof rowSchema>[] = [];
    let offset = 0;

    while (true) {
      let batch;
      try {
        batch = await Database.findAll({ offset, limit: BATCH_SIZE });
      } catch (err) {
        // Partial failure: return what we have so far instead of crashing entirely
        console.error(`getRecords: batch fetch failed at offset ${offset}, returning ${allRecords.length} records fetched so far.`, err);
        break;
      }
      if (!batch || batch.length === 0) break;

      const valid = batch
        .filter(r => r.id != null && r.status && r.status !== '')
        .map(r => ({
          id: r.id,
          orderId: r.orderId,
          dateOfLive: r.dateOfLive,
          page: r.page,
          liverName: r.liverName,
          locationOfMiner: r.locationOfMiner,
          minerName: r.minerName,
          itemDescription: r.itemDescription,
          grams: r.grams != null ? Number(r.grams) : undefined,
          source: r.source,
          category: r.category,
          mc: r.mc,
          goldRate: r.goldRate != null ? Number(r.goldRate) : undefined,
          supplierRate: r.supplierRate != null ? Number(r.supplierRate) : undefined,
          clientRate: r.clientRate != null ? Number(r.clientRate) : undefined,
          profit: r.profit != null ? Number(r.profit) : undefined,
          modeOfSale: r.modeOfSale,
          status: r.status,
          modeOfPayment: r.modeOfPayment,
          downpayment: r.downpayment,
          la1MonthPayment: r.la1MonthPayment,
          la2MonthPayment: r.la2MonthPayment,
          la3MonthPayment: r.la3MonthPayment,
          la4MonthPayment: r.la4MonthPayment,
          liverAdminRemarks: r.liverAdminRemarks,
          dispatchDate: r.dispatchDate,
          deliveredDate: r.deliveredDate,
          reviewChasing: r.reviewChasing,
          fbProfileName: r.fbProfileName,
          regions: r.regions,
          remittanceStatus: r.remittanceStatus,
          currency: r.currency,
          additionalCharges: r.additionalCharges,
          amountReceived: r.amountReceived,
          freeSf: r.freeSf,
          auditTrail: r.auditTrail,
          tog: r.tog,
          pureWeight: r.pureWeight,
          customerId: r.customerId,
        }));

      allRecords.push(...valid);

      if (batch.length < BATCH_SIZE) break;
      offset += BATCH_SIZE;
    }

    return allRecords;
  },
});

actions.content:
System Context & Goal:
Act as an expert React/TypeScript app developer, database architect, and UI/UX designer. Build an advanced, automated, role-based mobile web app for a jewelry business powered by a connected Google Sheet SDK. This must function as a comprehensive CRM, Order Tracker, and Invoicing system. The final product must be easy to use, visually clean, and precise, minimizing cognitive load for the staff while ensuring strict adherence to typography and branding guidelines.
CRITICAL REQUIREMENT: Upon generation, verify the app works by testing the switching between all 4 tabs, expanding client cards, inline editing details, and changing statuses without errors.

1. Core Architecture & UI/UX Principles

Mobile-First & Clean UI: Optimize heavily for mobile devices. Use whitespace effectively and intuitive navigation.

Role-Based Security: Secure login with strict permissions across 4 tabs: Admin Pipeline, Dispatch Board, Accounts Tracking, and Bossing Dashboard.

Global Action Button: Implement an omnipresent "Upload Masterlist" action button linking to the "Uploads" table.

Branding & Typography: * Use Cinzel Bold strictly for all headers, invoice titles, and highlighted metric text.

Use Lato for all body text, list items, data inputs, and general data.

Apply primary brand colors to buttons and active states.

Currency Conversion Automation (CRITICAL): AED is the default currency. The system MUST automatically convert foreign currencies to AED for all background calculations (profit, Remaining Balance, Executive Dashboards) using virtual fields. (e.g., If USD, multiply by 3.67. If PHP, divide by 15.5). Display original currency to the client, but use AED for company math. Ensure the Currency column is mapped.

2. Global Data Rules, CRM Logic & Validation

CRM Smart Client Bundling (CRITICAL): Visually merge flat rows with the same Miner Name into a single "Client Profile" (Parent Record) in all list views.

Precise Date Formatting: Format all dates strictly as short dates (e.g., "MMM DD, YYYY"). Strip out any raw ISO timestamp strings.

Automated Due Date: Create a virtual calculated field where Due Date = Date of Live + 3 Days.

SDK Type Casting: Ensure mc (Making Charge) and supplier rate are parsed to floats for math. downpayment is parsed as a number.

Smart Financial Calculation:

Item Price: If Grams contains text (like "PC"), Item Price = client rate. If Grams is a number, Item Price = (Grams * client rate).

Item Cost: If Grams contains text (like "PC") OR Category is "Silver", Item Cost = supplier rate + MC (Making Charge). If Gold by weight, Item Cost = (Gold rate * Grams) + MC.

Profit: profit = Item Price - Item Cost.

3. Role: Admin Pipeline (Frontline Processing & CRM Entry)

Filter Rule: Show only records where status is "Pending" or "Waiting for Details".

Grouping: Group lists by Miner Name (Client Profile view).

Visible Fields: Miner Name, Item Description, Category, Grams, Currency, status, mode of payment, Regions, Location of Miner, FB Profile Name.

Actions/Buttons (Speed Optimized):

NO separate Edit details tab/modal. Build inline editing directly on the client card/list view for mode of payment, Regions, and Liver/Admin Remarks so the admin can update instantly.

Conditional Validation Gate: Do not allow status to change to "For Pullout" UNLESS mode of payment is filled. AND, if Location of Miner strictly equals "Local", the Regions field MUST also be filled. (Ignore the region requirement for International or Pinas miners).

MOP Dropdown: Tabby, Tamara, COD, Bank Transfer, Credit Card, Meet Up, Pick Up Shop, Layaway.

Promo Toggle: Quick toggle for "FREE SF". Map this to the Free SF sheet column.

Quick Status Dropdown: "For Pullout" or "Cancelled".

4. Role: Dispatch Board (Logistics & Fulfillment)

Filter Rule: Show only records where status is "For Pullout" or "Dispatched".

Grouping (CRITICAL - SEPARATE LOCATIONS): Primary Group by Location of Miner (Local, Pinas, International) to separate shipping schedules. Secondary Group by Source (facility). Tertiary Group by Miner Name (bundled).

Automated Shipping Logic: Calculate virtual Shipping Fee based on this strict hierarchy:

If Free SF is checked/true -> Shipping Fee = 0.

If mode of payment = "Pick Up Shop" -> Shipping Fee = 0.

If mode of payment = "Meet Up" -> Shipping Fee = 10.

If Location of Miner = "Local" AND none of the above apply -> Dubai = 25; Western Region = 45; Abu Dhabi/Sharjah/Ajman/UAQ/RAK/Fujairah = 30.

Actions/Buttons:

Quick Status Change: "Dispatched", "Delivered", OR "Cancelled".

Generate Pullout Report (INTERNAL USE ONLY): Export a consolidated printable internal list. FILTER: ONLY include items where status is "For Pullout" (Do NOT include "Dispatched"). Group by Source. Include Date of Live, Gold rate, and profit. Do NOT format this as an invoice.

5. Role: Accounts Tracking (Financials & Layaway CRM)

Primary View Filter: Show records where (status is "Delivered" OR mode of payment is "Layaway") AND (Remittance Status is empty, "Pending Payment", OR Remaining Balance > 0).

Secondary View (Cleared Accounts): Separate sub-tab for records where Remittance Status is Secured/Received.

Surcharge & Extra Fees Logic: * CC Fee: If mode of payment = "Credit Card", CC Fee = 5. Otherwise, 0.

Calculated Fields:

Total Paid: downpayment + la 1 month payment + la 2 month payment + la 3 month payment + la 4 month payment + Amount Received.

Remaining Balance: (Item Price + Shipping Fee + CC Fee + Additional Charges) - Total Paid.

Actions/Buttons (Edit Financials Form):

Inputs: Gold rate/supplier rate, MC (Making Charge).

Manual Extra Entries: Map the "Additional Charges" input to the Additional Charges sheet column, and the "Amount Received" input to the Amount Received sheet column.

Layaway Conditional Logic: Disable/Lock the inputs for la 1 month payment through la 4 month payment UNLESS the mode of payment is exactly "Layaway".

Remittance Dropdown: Pending Payment, Secured via Courier, Secured via Bank Transfer, Secured via Cash.

6. Bossing Dashboard (Executive View)

Filter Rule: Show ALL records where status is NOT "Cancelled".

Date Range Control: Global Date Filter toggle (defaulting to "All Time").

Permissions: Strictly Read-Only. No charts/graphs, just clean data.

Top Metrics (KPIs in AED):

Total Expected Profit (Sum of all calculated profit for active/sold items, converted to AED).

Total Items Sold.

Outstanding Receivables (Sum of all Remaining Balances in AED).

7. Document Generation (Client Invoice)

Generate Invoice Button (CRITICAL): This must be entirely separate from the Dispatch Pullout report. Format as a professional receipt to send to the customer.

Header: Date of Live, Miner Name, Regions, mode of payment, Due Date.

Line Items: Loop bundled items. Show Item Description, Category, Grams, and client rate.

Totals Section: Subtotal + Shipping Fee + Additional Charges + (If Credit Card, +5 Surcharge). Display Grand Total.

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createEndpoint, Database } from 'zite-integrations-backend-sdk';

const rowSchema = z.object({
  id: z.number(),
  orderId: z.string().optional(),
  dateOfLive: z.string().optional(),
  page: z.string().optional(),
  liverName: z.string().optional(),
  locationOfMiner: z.string().optional(),
  minerName: z.string().optional(),
  itemDescription: z.string().optional(),
  grams: z.number().optional(),
  source: z.string().optional(),
  category: z.string().optional(),
  mc: z.string().optional(),
  goldRate: z.number().optional(),
  supplierRate: z.number().optional(),
  clientRate: z.number().optional(),
  profit: z.number().optional(),
  modeOfSale: z.string().optional(),
  status: z.string().optional(),
  modeOfPayment: z.string().optional(),
  downpayment: z.string().optional(),
  la1MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la2MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la3MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  la4MonthPayment: z.string().optional(),
  liverAdminRemarks: z.string().optional(),
  dispatchDate: z.string().optional(),
  deliveredDate: z.string().optional(),
  reviewChasing: z.string().optional(),
  fbProfileName: z.string().optional(),
  regions: z.string().optional(),
  remittanceStatus: z.string().optional(),
  currency: z.string().optional(),
  additionalCharges: z.string().optional(),
  amountReceived: z.string().optional(),
  freeSf: z.boolean().optional(),
  auditTrail: z.string().optional(),
  tog: z.string().optional(),
  pureWeight: z.string().optional(),
  customerId: z.string().optional(),
});

export default createEndpoint({
  description: 'Fetches all records from the Database sheet in batches',
  inputSchema: z.object({}),
  outputSchema: z.array(rowSchema),
  execute: async () => {
    const BATCH_SIZE = 500;
    const allRecords: z.infer<typeof rowSchema>[] = [];
    let offset = 0;

    while (true) {
      let batch;
      try {
        batch = await Database.findAll({ offset, limit: BATCH_SIZE });
      } catch (err) {
        // Partial failure: return what we have so far instead of crashing entirely
        console.error(`getRecords: batch fetch failed at offset ${offset}, returning ${allRecords.length} records fetched so far.`, err);
        break;
      }
      if (!batch || batch.length === 0) break;

      const valid = batch
        .filter(r => r.id != null && r.status && r.status !== '')
        .map(r => ({
          id: r.id,
          orderId: r.orderId,
          dateOfLive: r.dateOfLive,
          page: r.page,
          liverName: r.liverName,
          locationOfMiner: r.locationOfMiner,
          minerName: r.minerName,
          itemDescription: r.itemDescription,
          grams: r.grams != null ? Number(r.grams) : undefined,
          source: r.source,
          category: r.category,
          mc: r.mc,
          goldRate: r.goldRate != null ? Number(r.goldRate) : undefined,
          supplierRate: r.supplierRate != null ? Number(r.supplierRate) : undefined,
          clientRate: r.clientRate != null ? Number(r.clientRate) : undefined,
          profit: r.profit != null ? Number(r.profit) : undefined,
          modeOfSale: r.modeOfSale,
          status: r.status,
          modeOfPayment: r.modeOfPayment,
          downpayment: r.downpayment,
          la1MonthPayment: r.la1MonthPayment,
          la2MonthPayment: r.la2MonthPayment,
          la3MonthPayment: r.la3MonthPayment,
          la4MonthPayment: r.la4MonthPayment,
          liverAdminRemarks: r.liverAdminRemarks,
          dispatchDate: r.dispatchDate,
          deliveredDate: r.deliveredDate,
          reviewChasing: r.reviewChasing,
          fbProfileName: r.fbProfileName,
          regions: r.regions,
          remittanceStatus: r.remittanceStatus,
          currency: r.currency,
          additionalCharges: r.additionalCharges,
          amountReceived: r.amountReceived,
          freeSf: r.freeSf,
          auditTrail: r.auditTrail,
          tog: r.tog,
          pureWeight: r.pureWeight,
          customerId: r.customerId,
        }));

      allRecords.push(...valid);

      if (batch.length < BATCH_SIZE) break;
      offset += BATCH_SIZE;
    }

    return allRecords;
  },
});

// Backward-compatibility shim — re-exports from ratesStore
export { getPhpRate, setPhpRate, hasPhpRate } from '@/lib/ratesStore';
